_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: The 2026 Sun Belt Conferece has 14 teams now that Louisiana Tech has joined from Conference USA and Texas State has left to join the Pac 12.  Three storylines define the 2026 season: (1) Can top program James Madison continue their success with new HC Billy Napier?; (2) Can these programs continue their upper level G6 play after being raided by the P4 for their top coaches and best players, as only five teams rank above 105th in returning production? ;(3) .All G6 conference teams are dealing with the new portal era in their own way, but the reality is players that stand out at this level usually end up on a Power 4 roster the following season. Sun Belt Tier framework: When team_notes give a 3-tier range (e.g., Tier 2-4), that's not a hedge — it's the league norm. The agent should NOT over-anchor on a single 'centering' tier; instead treat the range itself as the projection. A team at Tier 2-4 means 'either it works and they're contending for the title game or it doesn't and they're fighting for a bowl' — both branches are real. Conference USA tier framework (tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record): Tier 1 = Sun Belt title-game favorite, lower tier CFB bowl game contenders ; Tier 2 = title-game contender, lower tier bowl game contenders; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game and bowl game outsider; Tier 4 = bottom of the conference where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be a tremendous achievement. When team_notes give a range, the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside — but per the volatility note above, 3-tier ranges are common here and the range itself should be framed as the projection rather than collapsing to a single tier. The Sun Belt is the only remaining conference that splits teams into Divisions (East and West), and the Championship game is played in the home stadium of the division leader with the best overall conference win percentage.

team_notes.app-state: Tier 2-4 Sun Belt East team. Signed their best recruiting class maybe ever, tops in the Sun Belt this year. However HC Dowell Loggains was just 5-8 in his first season at App St in 2025.  QB looks to be Malachi Singleton from Purdue (& Arkansas) to run new OC Mike Anthony's offense, who is from the Art Briles coaching tree. RB Jaquari Lewis is a good one and WR has some parts in Sm Mbake and portal adds Chris Lofton & Sam Pickett III.  OL is a huge question with only 2 with starting experience on the roster.  The DEs are decent but DT has a lot of questions.  Defense is lead by all SBC LB Colton Phares, and 11 portals were added to fix the secondary alone.
team_notes.arkansas-state: Tier 2-4 Sun Belt West team. Came up one win short of winning the SBC west in 2025. Return the most production in the Sun Belt at 74%, but lost star QB Jaylen Raynor to Iowa State in the transfer portal.  Lead by HC Butch Jones in his 6th season and his 26-37 record.  Four man QB race to start between Trey Owens, Ethan Crawford, Drew Dickey and Jeremy St-Hilaire. Red Wolves have really good skill players like RBs Kenyon Clay & Devon Spencer and great WRs like Chauncy Cobb and Hunter Summers.  OL has 5 with starting experience returning from the 2025 roster.  The defense is the big question, with a DL that added 7 portals and lacks answers on paper.  The secondary is the strength of the unit with guys like CBs Five Hamilton & Noah Flores, and safeties AG McGhee and Brandon Barnes. New OC Garrett Altman was Vanderbilt's QB coach and could really take off as long as QB gets sorted out.  Red Wolves could contend in the Sun Belt West.
team_notes.coastal-carolina: Tier 2-4 Sun Belt East team. New HC Ryan Beard was Missouri State's head coach and led them to a 7-6 season in their first year in FBS.  For starting QB, Deuce Bailey holding the spring edge over portal arrival Trever Jackson but will continue to battle for the starting role. The run game shows promise with a stable of four experienced RBs and an OL that has five players with starting experince, but 3 of which are new via transfer portal. New DC LD Scott's 4-2-5 defense saw a defensive line that made the most progress in the spring and should boast a decent secondary lead by CB Ja'Marion Wayne. Overall the defense added 19 portals to fix what was a bad unit last year. Fun nickname for the team are the Teal Chickens.
team_notes.james-madison: Tier 1 Sun Belt East team. 2025 Sun Belt Champions. Lost HC Bob Chesney to UCLA, hired Billy Napier who was fired from Florida.  Napier has a nickname of Sun Belt Billy after his success at Louisiana but failures in Gainesville. Lost QB Alonzo Barnett III to the portal to UCF.   JMU is the favorite to win the East and host the SBC Championship game.
team_notes.louisiana-tech: Tier 3-4 Sun Belt West team. First year in SBC, previously in CUSA.
team_notes.marshall: Tier 2-4 Sun Belt East team. Won 2024 Sun Belt championship under former HC Charles Huff.  HC Tony Gibson in his 2nd season.
team_notes.old-dominion: Tier 2-4 Sun Belt East team. ODU won 10 games for the first time in nine years in 2025, but fell short against JMU and missed out on the SBC Championship game.  HC Ricky Rahne is in his 7th season and has a 30-33 record. Lost dynamic QB Colton Joseph to Wisconsin via the transfer portal.  Jaxon Potter locked in at QB1 and Cure Bowl MVP Quinn Henicle behind him.
team_notes.southern-miss: Tier 3-4 Sun Belt West team. Lost HC Charles Huff after one season at So. Miss to Memphis, along with 16 of their key players from 2025 who transferred to Memphis along with Huff.  New HC is Blake Anderson who was sucessful HC at Arkansas St from 2104-2020, but was fired from Utah State after 2025 season after some ugly allegations. This team is a near-total rebuild: only 2 returning starters, only 31% of returning production on offense which ranks last in FBS, one of the thinnest overall returning-production profiles in FBS.  QB looks to be Ethan Hampton, a former starter at Northern Illinois.Should have a run game with RBs Pittman, Hood, Briggs and 4 OL portals that have starting experience.
team_notes.troy: Tier 2-4 Sun Belt West team. 2025 Sun Belt runner up, losing the SBC Championship game to JMU.  HC Gerad Parker led the Trojans to a 8-6 record in 2025 after a 4-8 record in his first season in 2024. Returning QB Goose Crowder took the spring reps as the projected starter with senior Tucker Kilcrease as experienced insurance.  OLL has experience but Troy allowed 59 sacks in 2025 (last in FBS at 4.21 per game), but the OL returns 5 with starting experience when adding in portals so they must improve.  New OC Adam Austin wants a quick release to protect QB & OL.  DE Donnie Smith a tremendous pass rusher, & CB Jonathan Pennix was great for Southern Miss in 2025 & safety  Justin Powe was a defensive standout and leader for Troy last year.  Troy ranks 2nd in the Sun Belt in talent ratings.
team_notes.ul-monroe: Tier 3-4 Sun Belt West team. Last FBS team to publish official roster on July 7th.  QB Armenta returns as the likely starter, can he take the next step in new OC Montalto's spread-RPO offense?  Can RB Chaney get healthy & regain form? As for the DL... new DC Tim Reffett's 3-3-5 should help cut down on needs. This DL has bodies but lots of unknowns right now. LB & Safeties have options and look like the strength of the defense.  Projects to be a bottom Sun Belt team.
